identity.canonical_title: Relation: Which Is Within ~1.5% of 1.1852. This May Be Coincidence, but One Can Imagine…

theory.canonical_statement: • Wave Modes and Half-Phases: The value 1.185 appears tantalizingly close to certain wave phenomena benchmarks. For example, the first zero of the Bessel function $J_0(x)$ occurs at $x\approx2.4048$, which is important in resonance of circular membranes and waveguides. Half of that value is $$\approx1.2024$$, which is within ~1.5% of 1.1852. This may be coincidence, but one can imagine a scenario where a "half-step" phase difference of about 1.18 radians (~68°) in a circular resonator produces a null or mode boundary - reminiscent of a half-wavelength condition. In the context of the user's DAT-GPT vortex theory, such a phase (close to $360^{\circ}/\pi\approx114.59^{\circ}$ or its complement) might be significant. Indeed, a "half-step Bessel drop" at ~1.18 radians was speculated as a point where a standing wave cancels out. Although not a standard textbook constant, seeing ~1.18 arise near a half of 2.4048 (and also near $π/2.64$) hints at a hidden harmony: it's as if an extra 18.5% tw
